subject: Joint Distributions
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ have the joint probability function $p_{XY}(x,y)$ described as follows: X\Y | 0 | 1 | $p_X(x)$; 0 | 1/18 | 3/18 | 4/18; 1 | 4/18 | 3/18 | 7/18; 2 | 6/18 | 1/18 | 7/18; $p_Y(y)$ | 11/18 | 7/18 | 1. Find $p_{X|Y}(x|y)$ and $p_{Y|X}(y|x)$.
answer: $p_{X|Y}(x|0) = \frac{p_{XY}(x, 0)}{p_Y(0)} = \begin{cases} 1/11 & x = 0 \\ 4/11 & x = 1 \\ 6/11 & x = 2 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{X|Y}(x|1) = \frac{p_{XY}(x, 1)}{p_Y(1)} = \begin{cases} 3/7 & x = 0 \\ 3/7 & x = 1 \\ 1/7 & x = 2 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ The conditional probability distribution for $Y$ given $X = x$ is $p_{Y|X}(y|0) = \frac{p_{XY}(0, y)}{p_X(0)} = \begin{cases} 1/4 & y = 0 \\ 3/4 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{Y|X}(y|1) = \frac{p_{XY}(1, y)}{p_X(1)} = \begin{cases} 4/7 & y = 0 \\ 3/7 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{Y|X}(y|2) = \frac{p_{XY}(2, y)}{p_X(2)} = \begin{cases} 6/7 & y = 0 \\ 1/7 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
explanation: Answer: $p_{X|Y}(x|0) = \frac{p_{XY}(x, 0)}{p_Y(0)} = \begin{cases} 1/11 & x = 0 \\ 4/11 & x = 1 \\ 6/11 & x = 2 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{X|Y}(x|1) = \frac{p_{XY}(x, 1)}{p_Y(1)} = \begin{cases} 3/7 & x = 0 \\ 3/7 & x = 1 \\ 1/7 & x = 2 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ The conditional probability distribution for $Y$ given $X = x$ is $p_{Y|X}(y|0) = \frac{p_{XY}(0, y)}{p_X(0)} = \begin{cases} 1/4 & y = 0 \\ 3/4 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{Y|X}(y|1) = \frac{p_{XY}(1, y)}{p_X(1)} = \begin{cases} 4/7 & y = 0 \\ 3/7 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $p_{Y|X}(y|2) = \frac{p_{XY}(2, y)}{p_X(2)} = \begin{cases} 6/7 & y = 0 \\ 1/7 & y = 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
